tasks.title: P1.1 Finalize platform architecture + ADRs

👤 HITL: Architect sign-off.

tasks.title: P1.2 Design normalized DB schema (Layers 1-3) + ERD

🎯 Goal: model auth + raw + normalized layers as the single source of truth.
📦 Scope: tables for experiences/projects/skills/... + relationship tables; ERD.
✔ Acceptance: ERD reviewed; covers spec §5.
👤 HITL: schema review.
🔗 Depends: P1.1

👤 HITL: migration dry-run reviewed.
🔗 Depends: P1.2

tasks.body:
🎯 Goal: enforce privacy at the database (not just UI).
📦 Scope: per-row policies for every user-scoped table; owner-only on raw/normalized.
✔ Acceptance: deny-by-default verified with RLS tests.
👤 HITL: security review of every policy.
🔗 Depends: P1.2

tasks.title: ORG.S6 Repo hygiene — untrack Chrome CDP cache (1901 files)
tasks.body:
🤖 AI-built this session — needs your review.
✅ Untracked .careerlens-chrome-cdp/ (1900 files) + stray submission .docx; files kept on disk. Added .gitignore guards (.careerlens-chrome-cdp/, *-chrome-cdp/).
Where: .gitignore (git index only; no commit made).
🗺️ Roadmap: unblocks clean diffs/PR reviews before any new platform work.
🟢 Best case (≤1wk): you commit the index change; board + repo stay clean.
🔴 Worst case: junk was already pushed to the remote → needs a history rewrite (git filter-repo) to fully purge.

tasks.title: ORG.S7 Points/Leaderboard/Growth requirements docs
tasks.body:
🤖 AI-built this session — needs your sign-off.
✅ Captured the confirmed two-track model (cut-throat merit leaderboard + diagnostic growth track, explicitly NOT equity-by-points), heap priority, clustered opportunity graph + tiebreaker, referral, link→JSON ingestion (deterministic vs AI), client-side browser-extension scraping.
Where: docs/ORG-OPERATIONS.md §10, docs/POINTS-ENGINE.md, docs/CLIENT-SCRAPING.md.
🗺️ Roadmap: spec basis that drives ORG.S8 (schema) + ORG.S9 (engine) + ORG.7/scraping.
🟢 Best case (≤1wk): you sign off → build proceeds unblocked.
🔴 Worst case: open questions (source weights, low-bracket cutoff, gain→difficulty) stay unresolved → engine tuning blocked.

tasks.title: ORG.S8 Points/referral/growth data model (Supabase + RLS)
tasks.body:
🤖 AI-built this session — needs schema review.
✅ 3 migrations + ERD. Append-only point_events ledger (service-role writes only), merit leaderboard matview (member_id + nickname only, no PII), anti-self-referral DB guards, growth = diagnostic only (no point redistribution).
Where: supabase/migrations/0001_org_activities.sql, 0002_points_leaderboard.sql, 0003_referrals_growth.sql, docs/DATA-MODEL.md.
🗺️ Roadmap: the org-ops DB foundation; pairs with ORG.10 (departments/voting).
🟢 Best case (≤1wk): applied to a real Supabase project; RLS deny-by-default verified.
🔴 Worst case: column-level privilege escalation (role/is_officer) needs an app-layer guard before go-live; matview refresh strategy untested at scale.

tasks.title: ORG.S9 Points engine (heap + merit leaderboard + growth diagnostic)
tasks.body:
🤖 AI-built this session — needs review + tests.
✅ Pure-logic TypeScript (no I/O): generic PriorityQueue (heap), source-weighted ledger aggregation, cut-throat merit ranking with a deterministic tiebreaker, growth recommendations that never change rank.
Where: platform/org-ops/points/ (heap.ts, scoring.ts, leaderboard.ts, growth.ts, index.ts, README.md).
🗺️ Roadmap: wire to the Supabase data-access layer (reads ORG.S8 ledger) + add unit tests.
🟢 Best case (≤1wk): tsconfig + vitest added, unit tests green, wired behind a repository.
🔴 Worst case: repo has NO TS toolchain yet → code is unverified (uncompiled, untested) until that exists.

tasks.title: ORG.11 Skills taxonomy — hybrid seed + emergent + HITL approval
tasks.body:
🎯 Goal: canonical skill set that grows from user achievements.
📦 Scope: ~20 preset seed skills + emergent candidates; admin/officer approves candidate→approved (HITL); aliases (JS→JavaScript); approved set IS the cache.
Where: supabase/migrations/0004_skills.sql, docs/POINTS-ENGINE.md §10.
🟢 Best (≤1wk): seed loaded, candidate→approve flow works.
🔴 Worst (2wk): alias normalization + cold-start noise needs tuning.
🔗 Depends: ORG.S8

tasks.title: ORG.12 Emergent skill discovery from achievements (candidate proposer)
tasks.body:
🎯 Goal: propose new skill candidates from user achievements.
📦 Scope: extract skill mentions → candidate rows + skills_refresh_log; never auto-approve (HITL).
Where: supabase/migrations/0004_skills.sql.
🟢 Best: candidates surface for review.
🔴 Worst: precision low → many junk candidates.
🔗 Depends: ORG.11

tasks.title: ORG.14 Categorical (per-skill) leaderboards — engine + matview
tasks.body:
🎯 Goal: rank members within each approved skill (on top of the overall merit board).
📦 Scope: skill-tagged points → skill_leaderboard matview; extend Python points engine for per-skill standings + tests.
Where: supabase/migrations/0004_skills.sql (skill_leaderboard), src/org_ops/points/.
🟢 Best (≤1wk): per-skill boards render w/ tiebreaker.
🔴 Worst: matview refresh cost at scale.
🔗 Depends: ORG.11

tasks.title: ORG.15 Competition ingestion — organizer (company/school) + required skills
tasks.body:
🎯 Goal: expand link ingestion to organizer + required skills.
📦 Scope: RAG over submitted link → competitions + competition_required_skills.
Where: supabase/migrations/0005_competition_intel.sql, platform/org-ops/ingest.ts, docs/COMPETITION-INTEL.md.
🟢 Best: organizer + skills extracted from a real link.
🔴 Worst: blocked on RAG infra.
🔗 Depends: ORG.1 (LinkIngestor), ORG.11

tasks.title: ORG.16 Previous winners + winner skill sets intel (public, gated)
tasks.body:
🎯 Goal: reference set of past winners + their skills.
📦 Scope: competition_winners + winner_skills from PUBLIC records.
🔗 BLOCKED: scraping infra (CLIENT-SCRAPING.md) + account/API access (user provides).
Where: supabase/migrations/0005_competition_intel.sql, docs/COMPETITION-INTEL.md.
🟢 Best: winners imported for one competition.
🔴 Worst: source ToS/dedupe blocks import.

tasks.title: ORG.17 Judges OSINT (public-only) + store for reference
tasks.body:
🎯 Goal: public professional intel on judges to prepare submissions.
📦 Scope: judges + judge_profiles (org/background/focus_areas, source_url).
⚖️ ETHICS: PUBLIC professional data ONLY; preparation not manipulation; respect ToS; HITL before storing (officer/admin read only).
🔗 BLOCKED: scraping infra + accounts.
Where: supabase/migrations/0005_competition_intel.sql, docs/COMPETITION-INTEL.md.
🔴 Worst: legality/ToS forces narrower sources.

tasks.title: ORG.18 Skill-based competition matching (high bracket → competitions)
tasks.body:
🎯 Goal: pick which high-bracket members to field per competition.
📦 Scope: competition_skill_match (required skills × per-skill standings, favor high bracket).
Where: supabase/migrations/0005_competition_intel.sql (competition_skill_match), src/org_ops/points/.
🟢 Best: ranked candidate list per competition.
🔴 Worst: sparse skill data weakens matches.
🔗 Depends: ORG.14, ORG.15

tasks.title: GOV.1 Chapter Proposal document
tasks.body:
✅ Authored by JohnAndrewBalbarosa. The chapter proposal document is written and ready for member review.
🗺️ Roadmap: feeds GOV.3 (members review) → GOV.4 (send to Sado & Shikari).
👤 So far only the author has worked on this project.

tasks.title: GOV.2 Chapter Bylaws document
tasks.body:
✅ Authored by JohnAndrewBalbarosa. The chapter bylaws document is written and ready for member review.
🗺️ Roadmap: feeds GOV.3 (members review) → GOV.4 (send to Sado & Shikari).
👤 So far only the author has worked on this project.

tasks.title: GOV.3 Members review + agreement on Proposal & Bylaws
tasks.body:
🎯 Goal: ALL chapter members review the Proposal (GOV.1) + Bylaws (GOV.2) and reach agreement/ratification.
👥 Audience: every member — add their GitHub usernames as assignees once available (no member list yet).
👤 HITL: members verify the documents (read/agree) before anything is sent.
🔓 Unlocks GOV.4 on agreement.
🔗 Depends: GOV.1, GOV.2

tasks.title: GOV.4 Send approved Proposal & Bylaws to Sado & Shikari
tasks.body:
🔒 LOCKED — unlocks only after GOV.3 (members' agreement) is Done.
🎯 Goal: send both approved documents (Proposal + Bylaws) to Sado and Shikari.
👤 HITL: a human confirms before sending (org-ops send gate).
🔗 Blocked by: GOV.3.
NOTE: GitHub Projects has no auto-unlock — when GOV.3 is Done, move this to Todo (manually or via gh).
